scenario: Which is appropriate physical exam in dashboard injury?
ai_notes: Dashboard injury causes posterior tibial sagging due to posterior capsule disruption and indicates posterior knee dislocation. Tibial sagging sign is more sensitive than other tests for this mechanism.
subject: ortho

scenario: A 75-year-old patient with chronic neck pain for 3 years. Pain radiates from neck to right index and middle finger. PE: decrease pinprick at 1st and 2nd fingers, decrease forearm and wrist extension power. Triceps areflexia, other WNL. What is the diagnosis?
ai_notes: C6 radiculopathy presents with thumb and index finger involvement. However, triceps areflexia (C7 reflex) and wrist extension weakness (C7 motor) with index/middle finger (C6-C7 dermatome) pain suggests C6-C7 level, likely C7 involvement. The answer should be C7 radiculopathy as triceps areflexia is the key finding.
subject: ortho

scenario: An 18-year-old man had motorcycle accident. PE: PR 80, BP 120/80, hip in internal rotation, flexion, neovascular intact. What is the diagnosis?
ai_notes: Posterior hip dislocation classically presents with hip flexion, internal rotation, and adduction (classic position). Anterior dislocation presents with external rotation and flexion.
subject: ortho

scenario: A 30-year-old man has motorcycle accident with 2 cm laceration and contaminated wound with muscle exposed on left leg. X-ray shows segmental tibia and fibula fracture. What is the most proper management?
ai_notes: This is a Grade III open fracture (>1 cm laceration with muscle exposed and contamination). Management priority is wound exploration for debridement before other interventions. This takes precedence over antibiotics alone.
subject: ortho

scenario: A 65-year-old woman presents with foot pain especially during push-off phase of walking. X-ray shows first metatarsophalangeal joint deformity. What is the appropriate surgical management?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Bunionectomy with soft tissue procedure (chevron procedure)

ai_notes: Symptomatic hallux limitus (limited first MTP joint motion) with functional pain during push-off requires both bunionectomy and soft tissue procedures (chevron or similar) to address both bone and soft tissue deformity.
subject: ortho

scenario: A 20-year-old female ballet dancer presents with posterior heel pain for 7 months following en-pointe dancing. Conservative treatment with medication and activity limitation for 3 months has not improved symptoms. What is the next management?

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debridement and removal of calcification of Achilles tendon

choices.label: E
choices.text: Extended conservative management with physical therapy

ai_notes: En-pointe dancers with posterior heel pain that fails conservative treatment typically have os trigonum impingement syndrome. Surgical excision of the os trigonum is the definitive treatment. Added option D and E as plausible distractors.
subject: ortho

scenario: An 18-month-old boy diagnosed with supracondylar fracture. After closed reduction and casting 8 hours, he cried and had pain at the casted arm. What is the appropriate management?
ai_notes: Pain after casting in first 24-48 hours may indicate compartment syndrome development. First step is to split the cast and add padding to relieve pressure. If pain continues or compartment pressure is elevated, fasciotomy is indicated.
subject: ortho

scenario: A 70-year-old woman presents with back and knee pain, joint stiffness and pain most occurring in the morning that improves in 15 minutes and aggravates again in the evening. PE: swelling and mild tenderness at 2 to 5 distal interphalangeal joints, full range of motion, no pain, crepitation on right knee movement. What is the diagnosis?
ai_notes: Morning stiffness lasting <15-30 minutes suggests osteoarthritis rather than rheumatoid arthritis (which lasts >1 hour). DIP joint involvement, crepitation, and improvement with activity are classic OA findings.
subject: ortho

scenario: A patient presents with numbness of the index finger and decreased function of the thumb. What is the diagnosis?
ai_notes: Index finger numbness with thumb dysfunction (opposition/flexion) indicates median nerve compression at the carpal tunnel. This is the most common presentation. Added options B-E as plausible nerve compression distractors.
subject: ortho

scenario: A patient presents with pain on the radial side of the wrist immediately after delivering her child. What is the diagnosis?
ai_notes: Postpartum radial-sided wrist pain is classic for DeQuervain tenosynovitis (inflammation of abductor pollicis longus and extensor pollicis brevis tendons). Positive Finkelstein test would confirm. Common in postpartum period due to repetitive hand use and hormonal factors.
subject: ortho

scenario: Which are the risk factors of developing osteoporosis?
ai_notes: Risk factors for osteoporosis include: European/Caucasian ethnicity (higher risk than African), sedentary lifestyle (under exercise), and low body weight (underweight). Obesity is protective. Over-exercise can prevent bone loss.
subject: ortho

scenario: A 2-year-old girl has delayed primary walking. At birth she had a skin defect at lower back with surgical closure at 11 months. She still cannot sit or crawl. Physical exam shows bilateral hip abduction and flexion contractures, bilateral clubfoot. What is the proper investigation?
ai_notes: History of lower back skin defect at birth suggests spina bifida. The neurological deficits (unable to walk, hip contractures, clubfoot) indicate cord tethering or significant spinal dysraphism. MRI is the gold standard for evaluating spinal cord abnormalities and tethering.
subject: ortho

scenario: A patient presents with comminuted fracture of right tibia and fibula. Physical exam shows pale right leg and absent dorsalis pedis pulse. What is the management?
ai_notes: Absent dorsalis pedis pulse indicates anterior tibial artery injury (the artery giving dorsalis pedis pulse). The pale, cold extremity suggests vascular compromise. Anterior tibial artery runs in anterior compartment and is most commonly injured in tibia/fibula fractures. Exploration and repair is indicated.
subject: ortho

scenario: A 15-year-old boy presents after trauma with a 15 cm laceration with soil contamination, cannot palpate distal pulse, cold extremities, and comminuted fracture of right distal femur. What is the Gustilo-Anderson grade and appropriate antibiotics?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Grade IIIB with cephalosporin and aminoglycoside

choices.label: C
choices.text: Grade IIIA with cephalosporin and aminoglycoside

choices.label: D
choices.text: Grade IIIC with cephalosporin, aminoglycoside, and penicillin

choices.label: E
choices.text: Grade IIIB with cephalosporin, aminoglycoside, and penicillin

ai_notes: This is Grade IIIC: >10 cm laceration, comminuted fracture, vascular injury (absent distal pulse, cold extremity), soil contamination. Grade IIIC requires triple antibiotic coverage: cephalosporin + aminoglycoside + penicillin (or high-dose clindamycin for anaerobes).
subject: ortho

scenario: A patient presents with numbness at the lateral malleolus area. Which test should be performed to determine the nerve root involvement?
ai_notes: Lateral malleolus area is supplied by L5 nerve root (superficial peroneal nerve). L5 motor function is tested by great toe dorsiflexion (extensor hallucis longus). L5 reflex is assessed clinically, though no specific reflex exists; ankle jerk tests S1.
subject: ortho

scenario: A child fell and used hand to break fall. On elbow exam, there is lateral stepping with displacement >2mm. What is the diagnosis and management?

choices.label: C
choices.text: Lateral epicondylar fracture, open reduction with pin fixation

choices.label: D
choices.text: Lateral epicondylar fracture, open reduction with plate and screw

choices.label: E
choices.text: Supracondylar fracture, open reduction with plate and screw

ai_notes: Lateral epicondylar fracture with displacement >2mm requires open reduction. The 'lateral stepping' refers to lateral displacement at the fracture site. >2mm displacement (some sources say >4mm) in a child requires open reduction and internal fixation, typically with plate and screw or pin fixation.
subject: ortho

scenario: An image shows finger deformity with PIP joint flexion and DIP joint extension. What is the diagnosis?
ai_notes: Boutonniere deformity shows PIP flexion and DIP extension. Swan neck shows PIP hyperextension and DIP flexion (opposite pattern). Boutonniere results from central slip disruption allowing lateral bands to slip volar.
subject: ortho

scenario: An X-ray shows the head of humerus is dislocated. Which is the least traumatic procedure for reduction?
ai_notes: Stimson technique (prone hanging arm) is considered least traumatic as it uses gravity and patient's own weight for reduction without aggressive manipulation. Kocher is more traumatic with multiple steps. Milch uses gentle traction and external rotation. Stimson is gentlest overall.
subject: ortho

scenario: A patient has a fracture of the distal 1/3 of humerus. Which test should be performed to assess nerve function?
ai_notes: Distal humerus fractures risk posterior interosseous nerve (PIN) injury. PIN innervates extensor muscles including extensor pollicis longus (thumb extension at IP joint). Thumb extension is the key test for PIN integrity. Finger extension (more proximal) tests posterior interosseous nerve more proximally.
subject: ortho

scenario: A patient presents with low back pain radiating to buttock and thigh to foot with positive straight leg raise test. What is the most appropriate next investigation?
ai_notes: Positive SLRT with radiculopathy symptoms (pain radiating to lower extremity) suggests nerve root compression. MRI is the imaging modality of choice for detailed visualization of disc hernia, spinal cord compression, and nerve root involvement.
subject: ortho

scenario: A 50-year-old woman presents with shoulder pain. She has no history of trauma. After pain subsides, she complains that her shoulder is stiff and she cannot move her hand over the head. What is the proper management?
ai_notes: This clinical presentation is classic for adhesive capsulitis (frozen shoulder). After the pain phase subsides, aggressive rehabilitation and intra-articular steroid injection are recommended. Immobilization would worsen stiffness. Early range of motion exercises combined with steroid injection is the standard approach.
subject: ortho

scenario: A 22-year-old female with history of SLE on prolonged steroid therapy presents with pelvic pain during walking. What is the most appropriate investigation?
ai_notes: Young female with SLE on prolonged steroids presenting with pelvic pain during walking raises concern for avascular necrosis of femoral head. MRI is the most sensitive imaging for early detection of AVN. The patient likely has femoral head AVN secondary to chronic steroid use.
subject: ortho

scenario: A male patient was struck by a car with leg swelling and pain on passive motion. X-ray shows non-displaced tibia fracture. Physical exam shows intact dorsalis pedis pulse. What should be done next?

choices.label: D
choices.text: Immediate open reduction and internal fixation

ai_notes: Swelling with pain on passive motion in the setting of tibia fracture raises concern for compartment syndrome. Although dorsalis pedis pulse is intact, compartment syndrome can develop with intact pulses. Compartment pressure monitoring is indicated; if elevated, fasciotomy is needed.
subject: ortho

scenario: A 50-year-old woman presents with plantar heel pain for 5 months. Pain is worse in the morning after a couple of steps of walking. Pain is aggravated by prolonged walking and standing. Physical exam shows tenderness at the medial calcaneal tubercle. What is the diagnosis?
ai_notes: Classic presentation of plantar fasciitis: morning pain with first steps (worse after rest), pain with prolonged standing/walking, tenderness at medial calcaneal tubercle (fascia origin). Heel spur is radiographic finding, not diagnosis. Achilles tendinitis causes posterior heel pain.
subject: ortho

scenario: A 22-year-old male presents after traffic accident with hip pain. Physical exam shows flexion, abduction, and internal rotation of the right hip. What is the diagnosis?
ai_notes: Posterior hip dislocation presents with classic triad: flexion, adduction, and internal rotation. The flexion with internal rotation (not external rotation) is key differentiator from anterior dislocation.
subject: ortho

scenario: A 10-year-old boy presents after motorcycle accident with right elbow pain and swelling. Physical exam shows swelling at the right elbow with loss of the triangular landmark at the elbow. What is the diagnosis?
ai_notes: Loss of triangular landmark (formed by lateral epicondyle, medial epicondyle, and olecranon) indicates disruption of normal elbow anatomy. In posterior elbow dislocation, the olecranon moves posteriorly and the triangle landmark is lost. This is the key finding for elbow dislocation.
subject: ortho

scenario: ผู้ป่วยหญิงปวดข้อ 2-3 วัน Joint aspirate: Cloudy, WBC 30,000 cells มี Rhomboid shape crystal ให้ยาอะไร
ai_notes: Rhomboid shape crystal = Calcium Pyrophosphate Deposition (CPPD/Pseudogout) ต้องให้ Colchicine ในระยะเฉียบพลัน
subject: ortho

scenario: ผู้ป่วยหญิง 40 ปี มีอาการปวดข้อเท้า มักเป็นตอนเช้า ต้องรอจนฟ้าสางแดดส่องจึงหาย PE symmetrical DIP joint ปวด จงวินิจฉัย
ai_notes: DIP joint involvement + morning stiffness + symmetrical = Rheumatoid Arthritis (OA ปกติไม่ DIP, SLE ผ้าคล้อง, Reactive arthritis ไม่ DIP)
subject: ortho

scenario: ผู้ป่วยมีประวัติ RA ที่ข้อมือและนิ้ว มา 10 ปี on ยาคุมอาการได้ดี ต่อมาปวดเข่าซ้ายมาก ขยับแล้วปวด มีบวม แดง ร้อน PE: Lt. knee tender on motion, swelling, redness, no fever v/s อื่นปกติ ปวดบวมที่ DIP, rt wrist; Lt Wrist swelling; not tender, no inflammation ที่ข้อนิ้ว การตรวจเพิ่มเติมที่เหมาะสมคือ
ai_notes: ลักษณะที่เข่าโดดเด่น (แดง ร้อน บวม) ขณะที่ข้ออื่นไม่มีอาการอักเสบ ดูเหมือนมี flare หรือ superimposed infection ต้อง joint aspiration เพื่อ differentiate RA flare จาก septic arthritis
subject: ortho

scenario: ผู้ป่วยหญิงอายุ 24 ปี on prednisolone 20 mg OD เพื่อรักษาอาการปวดข้อนาน 1 ปี จะพบความผิดปกติใดในผู้ป่วยรายนี้
ai_notes: Prednisolone 20 mg OD เป็นเบาะแส 1 ปี → ข้อเสีย: hypertension (sodium retention), hyperglycemia, osteoporosis (ไม่ increased), adipose tissue ฝาง cushingoid (ไม่ wasting), hypothyroid ไม่สัมพันธ์
subject: ortho

scenario: ผู้ป่วยชายอายุ 65 ปี U/D OA ต่อมามีอาการกดเจ็บบริเวณเข่าซ้าย มีปุ่มขึ้นมาบริเวณด้านหลังข้อพับ มีอาการตึงเข่า หลังตื่นนอน ตรวจร่างกายพบ mild swelling no redness no tender at medial aspect below patella 2 cm จงวินิจฉัย
ai_notes: ตำแหน่งกดเจ็บ medial aspect below patella 2 cm = anserine bursa (รวม sartorius, gracilis, semitendinosis tendons) No redness, no fever ตัดสิน infection/septic ออก
subject: ortho

scenario: ผู้ป่วยหญิงปวดแขนและไหล่ขวา 3 วัน เป็นมากเวลายกมือหวีผม ตรวจร่างกายพบจุดกดเจ็บที่ Tip of rt shoulder เจ็บมากขึ้นเวลา abduction จงวินิจฉัย
ai_notes: Pain on abduction + tenderness at tip of shoulder + lifting arm to comb hair = supraspinatus tendinitis (positive Neer test, painful arc 60-120°)
subject: ortho

scenario: ผู้ชายประสบอุบัติเหตุ ได้ NSAIDs แล้วอาการดีขึ้น แม่กังวลพามาพบแพทย์ PE: Loss of lumbar lordosis การจัดการเพิ่มเติมควรเป็นอย่างไร

choices.label: A
choices.text: ให้ NSAIDs ต่อ

choices.label: B
choices.text: ให้ NSAIDs เฉพาะตอนปวด

choices.label: C
choices.text: Rehabilitation exercise ที่บ้าน

choices.label: D
choices.text: Bed rest 1-2 สัปดาห์

ai_notes: Loss of lumbar lordosis ขณะอาการเฉียบพลันดีขึ้นแล้ว ต้องฟื้นฟูด้วย exercise/physical therapy เพื่อคืนสภาพปกติ
subject: ortho

scenario: ผู้ป่วยหญิงไทย 50 ปี ล้มศอกยันพื้น PE: ข้อมือขวาบวมผิดรูป จงให้การวินิจฉัย
ai_notes: Fall on elbow (actually fall on outstretched hand - FOOSH) + wrist swelling deformity = Colles' fracture (distal radius fracture, dorsal angulation)
subject: ortho

scenario: ผู้ป่วยมีอาการปวดคอร้าวลงแขน มีกล้ามเนื้อมืออ่อนแรง จึงตัดสินใจทา cervical traction 30 นาที/วัน ต่อมาอาการไม่ดีขึ้น จะทาอย่างไร

choices.label: A
choices.text: ให้ยาลดปวด

choices.label: B
choices.text: เพิ่มน้าหนัก traction

choices.label: D
choices.text: ลดระยะเวลา traction เหลือ 15 นาที

choices.label: E
choices.text: หยุด traction และปรึกษา neurosurgeon

ai_notes: Cervical radiculopathy with weakness ที่ไม่ตอบสนองต่อ traction ต้องทา imaging (MRI) เพื่อ differentiate cord compression/myelopathy จาก simple radiculopathy
subject: ortho

scenario: ชาย 60 ปี ปวดหลังบริเวณแอวขวา แถว TL junction มีกล้ามเนื้อแข็งเกร็ง ตรวจร่างกายพบ muscle strain ที่เอวขวา จะส่งตรวจเพิ่มเติมอะไร
ai_notes: อายุ 60 ปี + muscle strain ที่ TL junction ต้องเอก X-ray เพื่อ rule out fracture/pathology ที่ TL spine ก่อน ESR/CPK ซึ่งเป็น non-specific
subject: ortho

scenario: หญิง 70 ปี ลื่นล้มในห้องน้า 1 สัปดาห์ที่แล้ว เจ็บที่ขาหนีบขวา เจ็บมาก เดินไม่ได้ PE: External rotation and shortening ของขาขวา จงให้การวินิจฉัย
ai_notes: Fall + severe hip pain + external rotation and shortening = Fracture neck of femur (classic sign ที่เก่า)
subject: ortho

scenario: ผู้ป่วยหญิง collapse L-S spine จึงไปผ่าตัด จากนั้น 12 ชั่วโมง มีอาการเหนื่อยหอบ spO2 88% CXR: clear, ECG: sinus tachycardia จงวินิจฉัย
ai_notes: Orthopedic surgery (spinal collapse/fracture repair) + 12 hr postop + hypoxia + clear CXR + tachycardia = Fat embolism (CXR ปกติในช่วงแรก, petechial rash may appear)
subject: ortho

scenario: เด็ก 5 ปี fracture humerus ทา skin traction 2 วัน ต่อมามีปวดแขนด้านล่างมากขึ้น ปวดมากตอนกางนิ้วและกระดกนิ้วขึ้น จะจัดการอย่างไร

choices.label: A
choices.text: เพิ่มน้าหนัก traction

choices.label: B
choices.text: ให้ยาแก้ปวด + observe

ai_notes: Increased pain + pain with finger extension (active motion) = compartment syndrome sign ต้อง fasciotomy เพื่อ release pressure ก่อนเกิด necrosis
subject: ortho

scenario: After motorcycle accident, a young male aged 35 years presented with right groin pain. His film reveals 1 mm dislocation above right acetabulum. What is the proper management?
ai_notes: 1 mm dislocation (minimal displacement) = stable, can do closed reduction with percutaneous screw fixation to prevent late displacement
subject: ortho

scenario: Which one is not a clinical sign of acute compartment syndrome?

choices.label: E
choices.text: Pain on passive motion of distal joint

ai_notes: Acute compartment syndrome ที่ early stage อาจมี swelling, pain on passive motion, paresthesia แต่อุณหภูมิลดลงมักเกิดใน late/necrotic stage เท่านั้น หรือหากพูดถึง clinical sign อื่น absent pulse เป็น very late sign
subject: ortho

scenario: ตำรวจชายอายุ 30 ปี fall on his left shoulder พบไหล่ซ้ายปวดและบวม Radiograph พบ distal clavicle displace 1 mm above acromion process ถามว่า ligament ใดขาด
ai_notes: Distal clavicle displacement above acromion = AC joint dislocation → Coracoclavicular (conoid + trapezoid) + acromioclavicular ligament tear
subject: ortho

scenario: ผู้ชายยกของหนัก ได้ยินเสียง pop sound ที่มือขวา ขยับแขนข้างขวาแล้วปวด มี pop eye deformity ที่ forearm ลักษณะนี้เกิดจากการขาดของ
ai_notes: Pop eye deformity at forearm = Biceps tendon rupture (belly of muscle retracts proximally, distal forearm appears flat)
subject: ortho

scenario: Which is not a cause of neurological dysfunction in acute thoracolumbar injury?
ai_notes: All options except arterial occlusion directly compress/damage spinal cord. Arterial occlusion → ischemia → indirect mechanism (secondary not primary)
subject: ortho

scenario: A 55-year-old housewife has had right shoulder pain for 6 months. The pain is worsened when she hooks her bra and raises her hand above her head. PE: Trigger at upper part of shoulder, limited internal rotation and abduction. What is the likely diagnosis?
ai_notes: Chronic shoulder pain (6 months) + limitation of ROM (internal rotation, abduction) + trigger at upper shoulder + functional loss = Rotator cuff tendinitis/arthritis
subject: ortho

scenario: ชายอายุ 75 ปี อาชีพก่อสร้าง ขณะยกของหนัก เกิดเสียง pop sound and pain at right arm. There is swelling, ecchymosis and palpable deformity on the antecubital fossa. There is pop-eye deformity. What is the correct diagnosis and complication?
ai_notes: Pop eye deformity + antecubital fossa swelling = Biceps rupture → loss of supination (and weakened flexion)
subject: ortho

scenario: Burst fracture of thoracolumbar spine. Which statement is incorrect?
ai_notes: Burst fracture = retropulsion + vertebral body collapse → unstable (not stable) ต้อง surgical fixation หรือ conservative management with bracing ขึ้นกับ degree of kyphosis/neurological involvement
subject: ortho

scenario: ผู้ป่วยหญิงอายุ 50 ปี มาด้วยปวดบวมนิ้วเท้าซ้าย มีไข้ X-ray พบ cortical erosion, periostitis, tissue swelling น่าจะเป็นโรคอะไร
ai_notes: Fever + cortical erosion + periostitis = Osteomyelitis (bacteria destructive) not RA (periostitis rare) or septic arthritis alone
subject: ortho

scenario: Female 55 years present with right shoulder pain 6 months. Pain aggravated when hook her bra or abduct arm. Physical examination reveals tenderness at right upper shoulder, limited ROM when internal rotation and abduction. What is the most likely diagnosis?

choices.label: E
choices.text: TB septic arthritis of glenohumeral joint

ai_notes: Chronic pain + limited internal rotation and abduction + functional impairment (bra hook) = Rotator cuff tendinitis (not frozen shoulder which has global ROM loss)
subject: ortho

scenario: A 55-year-old male patient presented with numbness and weakness. Physical examination revealed straight leg raising test: negative; motor and sensory: within normal limits. X-ray found osteophyte at lumbar spine. What is the diagnosis?
ai_notes: Numbness and weakness but neuro exam WNL + negative SLR = asymptomatic finding (osteophyte incidental) or subclinical spondylosis, not true spinal stenosis (which would have positive neuro signs)
subject: ortho

scenario: A 70-year-old patient had left hip pain after fall for past 3 days. Examination reveals external rotation and abduction of left hip. The left buttock and upper thigh shows ecchymosis. What is the most likely diagnosis?
ai_notes: Elderly + fall + hip pain 3 days + external rotation + ecchymosis at buttock/thigh = Fracture neck of femur (classic presentation, not acute dislocation which present acutely)
subject: ortho

scenario: Which is the most common contracture of AKA (Above-Knee Amputation)?
ai_notes: In AKA (Above-Knee Amputation), the most common contracture is hip flexion and adduction. This occurs due to: (1) The pull of hip flexors (iliopsoas) which are stronger than extensors, (2) Adduction occurs from the stronger adductor muscles compared to abductors, and (3) Patient positioning in wheelchair/bed encourages flexion and adduction. Added option E (Hip extension and adduction) as a plausible distractor. Corrected option D spelling from 'roation IM' to 'external rotation'.
subject: ortho
